commands.review_summary:
The PR validates GitHub-backed ClawHub skill install resolver commits as full 40-hex SHAs before download, records the normalized commit in install metadata, and adds regression coverage for branch-like and padded resolver commits.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +69. Total +75 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main passes the resolver GitHub `commit` string directly into the codeload archive path, and the PR's mocked lifecycle test demonstrates the branch-like `main` case. I did not run a live ClawHub install in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human PR review should handle the contributor proof gate and the intentional custom-registry compatibility impact; there is no narrow remaining code repair to queue right now.

target.title: fix(clawhub): validate GitHub skill commit is an immutable sha before download
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The ClawHub GitHub-backed skill install path trusts the `commit` field from the install-resolver response and passes it straight into the GitHub codeload zip URL (`src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.ts` -> `downloadClawHubGitHubSkillArchive` -> `buildGitHubZipUrl` in `src/infra/clawhub.ts`) with no shape check. The resolver response is parsed as untyped JSON. A non-immutable ref (a branch or tag such as `main`) in that field would be accepted and fetched, so the commit-pinned source contract the type documents is not enforced on the path that downloads code.

A 40-hex sha guard (`normalizeGitHubCommitSegment`) already exists in this module but was only applied to provenance-display reads (`readVerifiedClawHubSkillSourceUrl`), not the install/download path.

## Why This Change Was Made

The resolver is an external boundary; the field that pins which code is downloaded and executed should be validated as an immutable commit sha there, consistent with the sibling provenance-display path that already requires it and the ClawPack download path that already fails closed on its artifact hash. This reuses the existing guard rather than adding a new one.

## User Impact

No change for normal installs: the resolver returns full commit shas, which pass through unchanged. A malformed or non-immutable ref now fails closed with a clear error before any network fetch, instead of silently fetching a moving target. No config, default, or migration impact.

## Evidence

`node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.test.ts`

Before (guard reverted) the added regression test fails because the install proceeds with `commit: "main"` and calls the downloader:

```
 ❯ src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.test.ts (75 tests | 1 failed)
   × rejects a GitHub-backed ClawHub skill resolved to a non-immutable ref
   AssertionError: expected true to be false
 Tests  1 failed | 74 passed (75)
```

After (with the guard):

```
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  75 passed (75)
```

The new regression test asserts that a non-immutable ref rejects (`ok:false`) before `downloadClawHubGitHubSkillArchive` is called.
